key_insight: PREDIKTOR predicts a patient’s drug response by aligning two complementary views: a personalized gene-regulatory knowledge graph and a simulated post-treatment transcriptomic perturbation profile.
clinical_relevance: The approach could help identify which cancer patients are more likely to benefit from a therapy before treatment, while also offering mechanistic explanations for the prediction.
method: Builds a patient-specific gene network from tumor expression and adds drug-target links from DrugBank.
Uses a graph neural encoder for a mechanistic drug-centered embedding and a frozen LINCS L1000 model to simulate perturbation profiles.
Aligns both representations with a CLIP-style contrastive loss and combines them for response classification.
evidence_signal: In TCGA evaluations across patient-, drug-, and tissue-split settings, the model outperformed state-of-the-art baselines and improved zero-shot AUROC by 5.6% on I-SPY2.
watch_item: Performance depends on high-quality transcriptomic input and knowledge-graph construction, so generalization may vary when data are sparse or patient biology is outside the training distribution.
paper_title: Predicting Therapeutic Outcome via Aligning Patient-Specific Knowledge Graph and Gene-Level Perturbation Representations
